newsletters.subject: The Trial That Could Break OpenAI
newsletters.rationale: Covers the Musk v. OpenAI trial that began April 27, 2026 in Oakland, with Musk testifying and seeking Altman's ouster. Also covers the Nippon Life Insurance Co. v. OpenAI case alleging ChatGPT practiced law without a license, plus OpenAI's $122B funding round, IPO strategy discussions, and geopolitical risks (Iran threatening Stargate data centers). Significant AI governance, legal precedent, and industry-impact content.
topics: Unlicensed practice of law via AI

newsletters.subject: The product skill you must now master: Reinvention

The actual article content was not accessible via extraction (redirected to app download page). The title suggests a general professional development piece about 'reinvention' rather than AI-specific technical content. No significant AI/LLM agent technical content detected.
newsletters.skip_reason: Non-technical career advice; no AI-specific content detected; links are mostly Substack UI elements

newsletters.subject: [AINews] The Inference Inflection
newsletters.rationale: Extremely high-value AINews from Latent.Space/swyx covering the shift from training to inference era. Key content: (1) Industry leaders (Noam Brown, Sam Altman, Jensen Huang, Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan) all signaling inference compute as strategic resource; (2) Coding agents evolving into platforms (OpenAI Codex with WebSocket mode, Cursor SDK as agent runtime, VS Code agent evaluation); (3) Agent Harness Engineering breakthrough (77% pass@1 on Terminal-Bench, HALO recursive self-improvement boosting AppWorld from 73.7 to 89.5); (4) New model releases (Mistral Medium 3.5 128B, IBM Granite 4.1 open-weight, Ling-2.6-flash 107B MoE with 61.2 SWE-bench, Tencent Hunyuan 1.8B-1.25bit); (5) Inference optimization (FlashQLA 2-3x speedups, vLLM on Blackwell 230 tok/s, LayerSplit 132% prefill throughput improvement); (6) Research on Incompressible Knowledge Probes showing factual knowledge scales log-linearly with model size (R²=0.917). This is dense with technical facts, model benchmarks, and industry shifts directly relevant to the AI agent wiki.

top_topics: Inference compute shift from training to inference era
New open-weight model releases and efficiency gains
Coding agents evolving into platform infrastructure
